title: MATH 341
text:
Course Materials

Required Course Materials




A First Course in Differential Equations, 3rd Ed. by J. David Logan, ISBN: 978-3-319-17852-3
A First Course in Differential Equations covers the basic topics in post-calculus differential equations. It is written for students in engineering, biosciences, physics, economics, and mathematics and emphasizes applications in those areas. Electronic access to A First Course in Differential Equations is available through the Weinberg Memorial Library. Access the book.






Required Textbook

title: Notes 1
text:
Goals
In this session, we will cover the following topics: 1. Define the notion of a differential equation and what is means to have a solution with an emphasis on the case of ordinary differential equations (ODEs). 2. Introduce the concept of an initial value problem (IVP). 3. Learn about some common situations where ODEs arise in applications.

title: Notes 1
section: The Concept of a Differential Equation
text: The Concept of a Differential Equation
